summary: Design for adding is_terminal_state(state: str) -> bool to lib.py — a pure stdlib helper returning True for the three terminal states (done, failed, blocked) and False otherwise (including non-strings and None without raising). One implementation task: add the function + a new focused test file tests/test_is_terminal_state.py with 8 unit tests.

ledger.what: Added PEP-3107 type annotations (state: str -> bool) despite existing lib.py having no annotations on any other function
ledger.why: The issue explicitly specifies the signature `is_terminal_state(state: str) -> bool`; adding annotations as requested avoids deviating from the stated contract
ledger.what_i_did: Added annotations exactly as specified in the issue; no other lib.py functions were annotated

blast_radius.why: Affects only the single new function; annotations are invisible at runtime and have zero impact on callers or the vendored engine unit

reversibility.why: Annotations are cosmetic; stripping them is a one-line change with no functional effect

ledger.revisit_if: The codebase adopts a no-annotation policy across lib.py or an explicit style guide prohibits annotations

ledger.what: Created a new file tests/test_is_terminal_state.py rather than extending an existing test file
ledger.why: The issue asks for 'focused pytest unit tests'; test_decide.py and test_correlation.py show a per-function self-contained file pattern for pure-function tests in this repo
ledger.what_i_did: Followed the existing per-function test-file pattern with a new self-contained file

blast_radius.why: Purely additive; no modification to any existing file; the new file is discovered automatically by pytest

reversibility.why: A new file; trivially deleted or folded into another file without touching any other code

ledger.revisit_if: The team wants all lib-function tests consolidated into one file

ledger.what: blocked is a terminal state in the same set as done and failed
ledger.why: The issue lists it explicitly; it needed verification that 'blocked' is indeed a terminal/final engine state and not just a label key
ledger.what_i_did: Verified against lib.py: 'blocked' appears in PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS (line 589) alongside 'done' and 'failed', and render_pipeline_status_body treats halted.reason == 'blocked' as a hard-stop final state; included 'blocked' in the terminal set

blast_radius.why: Read-only pure function; no mutations, no state writes; the terminal set is only consulted by callers who opt in

reversibility.why: Adding or removing a value from the terminal set is a one-line change; no downstream state is written

ledger.revisit_if: blocked is reclassified as non-terminal or a new terminal state is introduced
